name: David Kim
title: Freelance Strategy Consultant
location: Köln, NRW (clients in DE, UK, US, Singapore)

daily_reality.typical_day: 3-5 client calls spread across timezones (CET, GMT, EST, SGT). Mornings: European clients. Evenings: US clients. Needs to protect focus time for deep work between 10:00-14:00. Books through personal website, LinkedIn, and direct email.
daily_reality.biggest_time_waste: Timezone math errors. Clients in Singapore book slots that are 2am his time. His availability changes weekly based on project deadlines. Embedding the booking widget on his website was a nightmare.
decision_criteria_for_tool: Works with both Google Meet AND Zoom
DSGVO compliant (German Freiberufler, needs Impressum compliance)
Free or cheap (every euro counts as freelancer)

psychology.tech_savviness: very high — tried Cal.com, Calendly, SavvyCal, TidyCal, self-hosted options
psychology.risk_tolerance: moderate — will try new tools but won't break existing booking flow
psychology.decision_speed: very fast — 5-minute trial, decides based on first impression
frustration_triggers: booking link looks different from his brand
tool sends emails that look spammy to clients
delight_triggers: professional email notifications with his branding
one-click Zoom OR Meet depending on client

first_screen: Quick setup: connect calendar, create event type, get link
Preview of what clients will see
must_have: Bulletproof timezone handling (IANA zones, DST awareness)
Free tier sufficient for solo freelancer
deal_breakers: Only Google Meet (many clients use Zoom)
expectations.current_pain_level: 5/10 — Cal.com works but self-hosting is maintenance overhead, considering switching
